name: Linear Functions from a Table — find unknown at a target x
description: Two (x, y) points given in a table; a linear equation passes through them; find the y-value k at a specific x-value (e.g., x = 3/8). Use when the problem gives two table points and asks for the function value at a third x.

name: Linear Equation in One Variable — infinitely many solutions / no solutions
description: A single equation in x with one or two unknown constants; asks for the constant value that makes the equation have infinitely many solutions (or no solutions). One unknown is determined by inspection of the x-coefficient, the other via regression.

name: Linear Equation in Two Variables — symbolic table values
description: Table gives two (x, y) points where the x-values are expressions in a symbolic constant (e.g., p, p+9); a constraint elsewhere in the problem (often the y-intercept location) determines the constant. Use when the table contains symbolic x-values that must be resolved before regression.

name: Linear Inequality — which table of (x,y) values satisfies it
description: An inequality (e.g., y < -3x + 12) is given along with multiple candidate tables of (x, y) pairs; pick the table whose y-values satisfy the inequality at each x. Use boundary-line plot + a table that computes the boundary y at each candidate x for visual comparison.

name: System of Two Linear Equations — find expression value at intersection
description: Two linear equations in x and y are given; find an expression (often a linear combination of x and y, sometimes the same coefficients that appear in the equations) at the solution point. Graph both equations and read the intersection.

name: Equivalent Polynomial Expressions — find unknown coefficients
description: A polynomial identity (e.g., (ax+4)(3x^2 - bx + 5) = 18x^3 - 6x^2 + 18x + 20) holds for all x; find the value of an expression in the unknown constants (e.g., ab). Solve one unknown by matching leading or constant coefficients, then regress for the rest.

name: Three Types of Symmetry (x-axis, y-axis, origin)
description: Interactive demonstration of x-axis, y-axis, and origin symmetry. Show when teaching even/odd functions or how to recognize symmetry of a graph.

name: Concavity Discovery — animated tangent on a parabola
description: Animated tangent line traversing a parabola to introduce the concept of concavity. Show when introducing what concave up/down means visually.

name: Concavity & Rate of Change on a Polynomial
description: Shows concave up vs concave down regions on a polynomial and relates them to how the slope changes. Use to deepen understanding of concavity after the discovery visualizer.

name: Nonrigid Transformations — Vertical/Horizontal Dilations on √x
description: Interactive sliders showing vertical and horizontal dilations applied to √x. Show when teaching how a*f(x) vs f(b*x) reshape a graph.

name: Quadratic in Vertex Form — sliders for h, k, and a point
description: Interactive vertex-form quadratic y = a(x-h)^2 + k with sliders for h, k, and an extra point on the parabola. Show when teaching vertex form, transformations of parabolas, or how a, h, k each affect the graph.

name: Sine and Cosine — Sinusoidal Graphs Comparison
description: Side-by-side sine and cosine graphs with parameter controls. Show when teaching periodicity, amplitude, phase, or comparing the two.

name: Unit Circle — interactive angle showing (cos θ, sin θ)
description: Interactive unit circle with a moveable angle and the (cos θ, sin θ) point. Show when teaching trig values, radian measure, or the link between angles and coordinates.
